html:
Exclusive to:  

“Launches a tornado that moves around randomly damaging creatures and breaking structures.”

-

The Weather Pain is a ranged Weapon from the Reign of Giants DLC. It requires 10 Down Feathers, 1 Volt Goat Horn, and 1 Gears to craft, and an Alchemy Engine to prototype.

When used, it launches a whirlwind toward the target which will dance back and forth near the point of impact once it reaches the target, damaging enemies and destroying objects it comes in contact with.

Because the Weather Pain's projectile is not affected by the player, the Weather Pain is not affected by damage multipliers and will always do up to 42 damage/second when used by any character.

Skins

Main article: Belongings

Woven - Elegant
Feather Vane
Give your attacks a murderous twist!

Tip

  • Due to the somewhat unpredictable behavior of its projectile, the Weather Pain is best used against slow or immobile targets that spawn weaker mobs to defend them, e.g., Big Tentacles, Poison Birchnut Trees, or Spider Queens. This is especially effective against Poison Birchnut Trees, which will die in one hit, making the Weather Pain an ideal tool for acquiring Living Log.
  • Against particularly slow Mobs or even immobile ones, the Weather Pain can do more than 100 damage with a single use, surpassing even Blow Darts, making it potentially the best ranged weapon—depending on the target.
  • The Weather Pain's projectile destroys trees, which can make it useful for gathering Logs, especially if the trees are planted close together. This will often happen inadvertently in a Poison Birch farm.
  • By using a Construction Amulet to make one using 5 feathers, and then using a Deconstruction Staff to get ten feathers back, the Weather Pain can be renewed easily in late game as long as the player doesn't use all its charges before deconstructing it.
  • The Weather Pain is handy against certain Boss Mobs such as the Toadstool's Sporecaps and Ancient Fuelweaver's Woven Shadows.

Trivia

  • The name "Weather Pain" is a portmanteau of the words "Weather Vane" and "Pain".
  • Weather pains in real life are joint pains induced by atmospheric pressure changes.
  • A Shadow Creature that is able to be seen but not yet aggressive towards the players can be aggroed indirectly with the Weather Pain. This can be achieved by using it on some structure or creature which is on the other side of the Shadow Creature. When the projectile is launched, it is able to interact with the Shadow Creature before hitting the other target. This causes it to be aggressive towards the player for a short time.
  • Due to the fact that the Moose Goose only drops up to 5 down feathers, it's impossible to craft this without fighting the former twice, fighting Moslings, or using a Construction Amulet.
  • The Weather Pain is featured as a Magic weapon in Terraria as a part of the Terraria x Don't Starve event.

Bugs

  • If the Shadow Puppets summoned with Maxwell's Codex Umbra are hit by this weapon's projectile, they will become hostile.
  • If a Shadow Creature is hit by this weapon's projectile, it will become hostile to the player, regardless of Sanity level.
  • If a static Character (a player character summoned by commands) is hit by this projectile, it will be briefly hostile.
  • If Abigail is hit by this weapon's projectile, she will try to attack and no longer follow Wendy.

Assets

Weather PainFeather Vane

  • Dropped
  • Equipped
  • Tornado

  • Dropped
  • Equipped
  • Tornado

Gallery

  • The Weather Pain's whirlwind after destroying a Poisonous Birchnut Tree.
  • The Weather Pain being used against a Big Tentacle. The whirlwind will make quick work of the Baby Tentacles.
  • Weather Pain in Terraria
Tools and WeaponsCraftable Items

up to 42/sec for 5 sec.

"staff_tornado"
“Spinning doom.”
–Wilson
“Always whirling! Swirling towards destruction!”
–Willow
“Spin and spin!”
–Wolfgang
“Some weather out there.”
–Wendy
“TREACHEROUSLY POWERFUL GUSTS”
–WX-78
“I've never seen a weather vane employed as a weapon.”
–Wickerbottom
“I prefer to chop the trees myself.”
–Woodie
“Wind power.”
–Maxwell
“Where does the air current originate?”
–Wagstaff
“A störm öf pain.”
–Wigfrid
“We'll huff and we'll puff.”
–Webber
“I'm glad I'm not on the receiving end.”
–Walani
“Does nature like being tamed?”
–Warly
“Whut unholy nonsense be 'tis?”
–Woodlegs
“MAKE'TH BIG WINDS”
–Wilba
“Makes big winds!”
–Wormwood
“I think I'll see which way the wind blows before I use this.”
–Wheeler
“All bluster, no bite.”
–Winona
“Swirl and twirl, let cruel winds unfurl!”
–Wortox
“Make wind go spinny!”
–Wurt
“What a pane... heh.”
–Walter
“Oh botheration, I thought it was a compass.”
–Wanda
“Launches a tornado that moves around randomly damaging creatures and breaking structures.”
-
The Weather Pain is a ranged Weapon from the Reign of Giants DLC. It requires 10 Down Feathers, 1 Volt Goat Horn, and 1 Gears to craft, and an Alchemy Engine to prototype.
When used, it launches a whirlwind toward the target which will dance back and forth near the point of impact once it reaches the target, damaging enemies and destroying objects it comes in contact with.
Because the Weather Pain's projectile is not affected by the player, the Weather Pain is not affected by damage multipliers and will always do up to 42 damage/second when used by any character.

Feather Vane
Give your attacks a murderous twist!
Tip[edit | edit source]
Due to the somewhat unpredictable behavior of its projectile, the Weather Pain is best used against slow or immobile targets that spawn weaker mobs to defend them, e.g., Big Tentacles, Poison Birchnut Trees, or Spider Queens. This is especially effective against Poison Birchnut Trees, which will die in one hit, making the Weather Pain an ideal tool for acquiring Living Log.
Against particularly slow Mobs or even immobile ones, the Weather Pain can do more than 100 damage with a single use, surpassing even Blow Darts, making it potentially the best ranged weapon—depending on the target.
The Weather Pain's projectile destroys trees, which can make it useful for gathering Logs, especially if the trees are planted close together. This will often happen inadvertently in a Poison Birch farm.
By using a Construction Amulet to make one using 5 feathers, and then using a Deconstruction Staff to get ten feathers back, the Weather Pain can be renewed easily in late game as long as the player doesn't use all its charges before deconstructing it.
The Weather Pain is handy against certain Boss Mobs such as the Toadstool's Sporecaps and Ancient Fuelweaver's Woven Shadows.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
The name "Weather Pain" is a portmanteau of the words "Weather Vane" and "Pain".
Weather pains in real life are joint pains induced by atmospheric pressure changes.
A Shadow Creature that is able to be seen but not yet aggressive towards the players can be aggroed indirectly with the Weather Pain. This can be achieved by using it on some structure or creature which is on the other side of the Shadow Creature. When the projectile is launched, it is able to interact with the Shadow Creature before hitting the other target. This causes it to be aggressive towards the player for a short time.
Due to the fact that the Moose Goose only drops up to 5 down feathers, it's impossible to craft this without fighting the former twice, fighting Moslings, or using a Construction Amulet.
The Weather Pain is featured as a Magic weapon in Terraria as a part of the Terraria x Don't Starve event.

If the Shadow Puppets summoned with Maxwell's Codex Umbra are hit by this weapon's projectile, they will become hostile.
If a Shadow Creature is hit by this weapon's projectile, it will become hostile to the player, regardless of Sanity level.
If a static Character (a player character summoned by commands) is hit by this projectile, it will be briefly hostile.
If Abigail is hit by this weapon's projectile, she will try to attack and no longer follow Wendy.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Cơn Đau Thời Tiết là một Vũ Khí tầm xa trong DLC ​​Reign of Giants. Nó cần 10 Lông tơ, 1 Vôn sừng dê và 1 Bánh răng để chế tạo và Máy luyện kim để tạo nguyên mẫu.
title: Weather Pain/DST

It requires 10 [[Down Feather/DST|Down Feathers]], 1 [[Volt Goat Horn/DST|Volt Goat Horn]], and 1 [[Gears/DST|Gears]] to craft, and an [[Alchemy Engine/DST|Alchemy Engine]] to prototype. Because the Weather Pain's projectile is not affected by the player, the Weather Pain is not affected by damage multipliers and will always do up to 42 damage/second when used by any character. __NOTOC__ == [[File:Skins Icon.png|32px|link=]] Skins == {{Main|Belongings#Weapons Skins|Belongings}}

== {{Pic32|Prototype}} Tip == * Due to the somewhat unpredictable behavior of its projectile, the Weather Pain is best used against slow or immobile targets that spawn weaker mobs to defend them, e.g., [[Big Tentacle/DST|Big Tentacles]], [[Poison Birchnut Tree/DST|Poison Birchnut Trees]], or [[Spider Queen/DST|Spider Queens]]. This is especially effective against Poison Birchnut Trees, which will die in one hit, making the Weather Pain an ideal tool for acquiring [[Living Log/DST|Living Log]]. * Against particularly slow [[Mobs/DST|Mobs]] or even immobile ones, the Weather Pain can do more than 100 damage with a single use, surpassing even [[Blow Dart/DST|Blow Darts]], making it potentially the best ranged weapon—depending on the target. * The Weather Pain's projectile destroys trees, which can make it useful for gathering [[Log/DST|Logs]], especially if the trees are planted close together. This will often happen inadvertently in a Poison Birch farm. * By using a [[Construction Amulet/DST|Construction Amulet]] to make one using 5 feathers, and then using a [[Deconstruction Staff/DST|Deconstruction Staff]] to get ten feathers back, the Weather Pain can be renewed easily in late game as long as the player doesn't use all its charges before deconstructing it. *The Weather Pain is handy against certain [[:Category:Boss Monsters|Boss Mobs]] such as the Toadstool's Sporecaps and Ancient Fuelweaver's Woven Shadows. == {{Pic32|Placeholder}} Trivia == * The name "Weather Pain" is a portmanteau of the words "Weather Vane" and "Pain". * [[Wikipedia:Weather_pains|Weather pains]] in real life are joint pains induced by atmospheric pressure changes. * A Shadow Creature that is able to be seen but not yet aggressive towards the players can be aggroed indirectly with the Weather Pain. This can be achieved by using it on some structure or creature which is on the other side of the Shadow Creature. When the projectile is launched, it is able to interact with the Shadow Creature before hitting the other target. This causes it to be aggressive towards the player for a short time. * Due to the fact that the [[Moose/Goose/DST|Moose Goose]] only drops up to 5 down feathers, it's impossible to craft this without fighting the former twice, fighting [[Mosling]]s, or using a [[Construction Amulet/DST|Construction Amulet]]. * The Weather Pain is [[wgg:terraria:Weather_Pain|featured]] as a Magic weapon in Terraria as a part of the ''Terraria x Don't Starve'' event. == {{Pic32|Mosquito}} Bugs == * If the Shadow Puppets summoned with Maxwell's [[Codex Umbra/DST|Codex Umbra]] are hit by this weapon's projectile, they will become hostile. * If a [[Shadow Creature]] is hit by this weapon's projectile, it will become hostile to the player, regardless of Sanity level. * If a static [[Character]] (a player character summoned by commands) is hit by this projectile, it will be briefly hostile. * If [[Abigail/DST|Abigail]] is hit by this weapon's projectile, she will try to attack and no longer follow Wendy. == {{Pic32|Research Notes}} Assets == Weather Pain= Weather Pain Dropped.png|Dropped Weather Pain Equipped.png|Equipped Weather Pain Tornado.gif|Tornado |-| Feather Vane= Feather Vane_Dropped.png|Dropped Feather Vane_Equipped.png|Equipped Feather Vane Tornado.gif|Tornado == {{Pic32|Blueprint}} Gallery == Weather Pain vs. Birchnut Tree.png|The Weather Pain's whirlwind after destroying a Poisonous Birchnut Tree.
